story.text: As the loop's owner, I want the remaining fixed-verdict mock gates (handoff-gate, value-confirm) de-mocked under admitted, rung-checked backings — inference or deterministic per the question each gate actually asks — and 'the five gates' enumerated once on the record, so the ambient hook's mock temperature reads 0/5 and every gate carries at least one non-accept verdict in its history. A gate that has never refused is a mock with a bigger bill (report 0004); first-light proved the value gate can say no (rcp.3b62807d5f6a), and this atom carries that proof across the rest of the pipeline.

_note: v2 re-versions the atom now that handoff-gate is real (adm.b04f3b55114f, on bdo's 'yes/make it real' stamp). It is the same work item, and it carries a real structural gap the mocks always hid: its incidence.hands_off_to is empty — it declares no downstream for the finished work. The mock handoff gate stamped ready_for_spec over that gap (v0); the real L2 gate must read it as send_back. That refusal is handoff's first light: the third real gate saying no on the live log, and the proof it judges structure rather than waving everything through. The recursion holds — the atom about 'every gate must refuse' is itself refused, now by handoff, for being hollow.
